div.div: Request for ballot feedback: We would like the 'SHOULD' assertions above to become 'SHALL' assertions. We are invoking our usual implementer outreach process to find out whether anyone else objects, but please file tickets for any rules you do not believe can be enforced that way.

div.p: Changes since the last ballot: SearchParameter widened how it names resource types - SearchParameter.base and SearchParameter.target are now uri rather than code, so a search parameter can be defined against a logical model or other canonical resource and not only a named resource type. Jurisdiction element(s) are no longer deprecated (they keep an explanatory note), and the definitions of SearchParameter.type and of the missing-value semantics for multipleOr / multipleAnd were clarified.

ul.li: Type of SearchParameter.base changed from code to uri, allowing a logical model or other canonical resource to be named as the base rather than only a resource-type code [FHIR-51651]
Type of SearchParameter.target changed from code to uri, same rationale [FHIR-51651]
Jurisdiction element(s) are no longer deprecated but retain an explanatory note [FHIR-53766]
Clarified the handling of SearchParameter.type in response to the request that it “has no escape valve” - a clarifying note was applied rather than adding a new type option [FHIR-55190]
Clarified the meaning when SearchParameter.multipleOr and SearchParameter.multipleAnd are absent [FHIR-40328]

div.p: A SearchParameter resource specifies a search parameter that may be used on the RESTful API to search or filter on a resource. The SearchParameter resource declares:

ul.li: how to typically refer to the search parameter from a client (SearchParameter.code)
how the search parameter is to be understood by the server
where in the source resource the parameter matches
Search Parameters are referred to by CapabilityStatement resources via the canonical URL for a search parameter (CapabilityStatement.rest.resource.searchParam.definition)
Search Parameters can appear in an Implementation Guide to specify how resources can be found

div.p: Implementers should be familiar with the background and concepts described in Search on the RESTful API before working with this resource.
There is a registry of all Search Parameters.
